title: How to Download Voter ID Card (e-EPIC) Online — Register & Check Status
meta_description: Voter ID e-EPIC 2026 — download digital voter card from voters.eci.gov.in, new voter registration Form 6, name check, and EPIC card application.

hero.one_liner: Download your digital e-EPIC voter ID or register as a new voter if you've turned 18 — Election Commission portal with instant access

sections.heading: What is e-EPIC and Why You Need It
content: e-EPIC: Digital (electronic) version of your Voter ID card. It's a PDF downloaded directly from the Election Commission portal.
Officially valid as voter identity proof for voting and other government ID purposes. Can be shown on mobile or printed.
Traditional EPIC: The physical plastic card mailed to your address. Takes 30-45 days to arrive.

Both e-EPIC and physical card serve the same purpose. Why Get It Now? 
(1) Voting: You MUST be registered to vote in elections.
Without registration, you can't vote. (2) Government ID: Voter ID is accepted as government-issued identity proof for: bank accounts, passport, PAN card, insurance, loans.
(3) Scholarship eligibility: Many government scholarships require voter ID as proof of citizenship. (4) Pension/benefit claims: Some government schemes require voter ID for eligibility verification.

(5) Quick download: e-EPIC is instant — no waiting for physical card. WHO CAN REGISTER:
- Indian citizen
- 18 years or older on the 'qualifying date' (January 1 of the year).

If you turn 18 by Jan 1, 2026, you can register for 2026 elections.
- Usual resident of the constituency where you want to register
- NOT a non-citizen, convicted of certain crimes, or declared unsound mind by court

sections.heading: How to Download e-EPIC If Already Registered

steps.title: Visit voters.eci.gov.in (NVSP Portal)
steps.description: Go to National Voter Service Portal. Click 'Login/Register' on top right. Register with your mobile number if first time visiting. Verify mobile via OTP. Once verified, you can login anytime.

steps.title: Login to your account
steps.description: Enter your mobile number + OTP (sent to your phone). You're now logged into your voter dashboard.

steps.title: Go to 'Download e-EPIC'
steps.description: On your dashboard, click 'Download e-EPIC'. Alternatively, look for 'e-EPIC' or 'Digital Voter ID' option in the menu.

steps.title: Search for your name in voter roll
steps.description: Enter your details to search: EPIC number (voter ID, if you know it) OR your full name + father's/mother's name + date of birth + constituency. The portal searches the electoral roll and finds your record.

steps.title: Verify your details match
steps.description: Review the details shown: Your full name, date of birth, gender, father's/mother's name, address, constituency, serial number, and EPIC number. Ensure everything is correct. If details are wrong, you'll need to apply for correction first (see FAQs).

steps.title: Request e-EPIC
steps.description: Click 'Download e-EPIC'. You'll receive an OTP on your registered mobile to verify it's actually you requesting the download (security measure).

steps.title: Enter OTP and download PDF
steps.description: Enter the OTP. Your e-EPIC PDF is generated and available for immediate download. The PDF contains: your photo, name, EPIC number, date of birth, constituency, and a QR code for verification.

steps.title: Save and use as needed
steps.description: Save the PDF on your device. You can: (1) Print it for voting or government ID purposes, (2) Show on mobile at polling station, (3) Share for online KYC/verification, (4) Email to any institution requesting voter ID. It's legally valid everywhere.

sections.heading: How to Register as a New Voter (Form 6)

steps.title: Visit voters.eci.gov.in → 'Register as New Voter'
steps.description: Go to the portal. Click 'New Voter Registration' or 'Register to Vote'. You'll be directed to the Form 6 online application. Create account if first time, or login with existing account.

steps.title: Fill Form 6 application online
steps.description: Enter: Full name, Date of birth, Gender, Father's/Mother's/Husband's name (as applicable), Current address, Permanent address (if different), Constituency (auto-detected from address), Contact mobile number, Email. The form is straightforward — takes 5-10 minutes.

steps.title: Verify age requirement
steps.description: You MUST be 18 years or older on the qualifying date (January 1 of the current year). If you're 17 but will turn 18 by Jan 1, 2026, you can register for 2026 elections. The portal verifies this automatically.

steps.title: Upload documents
steps.description: Age proof: Aadhaar card (best), Birth certificate, 10th marksheet with DOB, Passport, or Driving License. Address proof: Aadhaar card, Utility bill (electricity/water/gas, within 3 months), Bank passbook/statement, Passport, or Voter ID from another state. Passport-size color photo (JPEG, 10x12 cm approximately). All documents should be clear scans/photos, preferably PDF.

steps.title: Submit Form 6
steps.description: Review all entered details. Ensure documents are properly uploaded. Click 'Submit'. You'll get a reference number — save it to track your application.

steps.title: BLO verification (Booth Level Officer)
steps.description: After you submit, the Booth Level Officer (BLO) assigned to your polling booth reviews your application. They may visit your address to verify you actually live there, or ask you to visit their office with original documents. This is standard verification to prevent ghost voters.

steps.title: Your name added to electoral roll
steps.description: After successful BLO verification, your name is added to the electoral roll (voter list). You'll receive notification via email/SMS. Your EPIC number is generated. e-EPIC can be downloaded from voters.eci.gov.in immediately. Physical EPIC card is mailed to your address within 30-45 days (or collected from BLO office).

sections.heading: How to Check If Your Name is in Voter List

steps.title: Visit voters.eci.gov.in → 'Search in Electoral Roll'
steps.description: Go to the portal. Click 'Search in Electoral Roll' or 'Check Your Name'. This is a public search (no login needed) to verify if someone is registered to vote.

steps.title: Enter your details and search
steps.description: Enter: Your name + State + District + Assembly Constituency. OR enter EPIC number directly. The portal searches against the published electoral roll.

steps.title: View results
steps.description: If you're registered, the result shows: Your name, EPIC number, date of birth, address, constituency, and serial number in the roll. If not registered, you'll see 'No records found' — then apply for new registration via Form 6.

steps.title: Alternative ways to check
steps.description: SMS: Send 'EPIC <your EPIC number>' to 1950. Voter Helpline App: Download from Google Play Store / App Store (free app by Election Commission). App allows checking name, printing e-EPIC, updating details, and applying for corrections.

sections.heading: Electoral Roll & Qualifying Dates
content: Electoral Roll is updated periodically. The Election Commission has introduced 4 QUALIFYING DATES in a year: January 1, April 1, July 1, October 1.

This means you can register as a new voter 4 times a year (compared to previously just once a year). Qualifying Date Explanation: If you turn 18 on or before a qualifying date, you're eligible to register for that date's electoral roll updates.

For example: If you turn 18 on March 15, 2026, you can register on the APRIL 1 qualifying date (since March 15 is between Jan 1 and April 1). Before Elections: The Election Commission publishes DRAFT electoral rolls 2-3 months before elections.
This draft is open for objections/corrections for 15-30 days. You can: (1) Check if your name is in the draft roll, (2) Object if you find errors, (3) Apply for inclusion if your name is missing.
After objection period, FINAL roll is published. Missing from Final Roll?
If you applied but your name doesn't appear in final roll before elections, you cannot vote in that election. However, you can challenge this or apply after elections (for next election cycle).

sections.heading: EPIC Card Types & Validity
rows: Mailed within 30-45 days or collect from BLO office
Only if you've linked Aadhaar with voter ID

faqs.question: I lost my EPIC card. How to get a duplicate?
faqs.answer: Your e-EPIC is permanent — you can download it anytime from voters.eci.gov.in for free. You don't need the physical card to vote (e-EPIC works). If you want a duplicate physical card, apply at your BLO office (Booth Level Officer) or through voters.eci.gov.in 'Request for Duplicate EPIC'. Processing: 15-30 days.

faqs.question: Can I vote with e-EPIC on my phone?
faqs.answer: YES. The e-EPIC PDF downloaded from the portal is legally accepted as voter identity proof at polling stations. You can show it on your mobile phone (just unlock your PDF) or carry a printed copy. Any valid government-issued ID (Aadhaar, Passport, Driving License) also works for voting — you don't necessarily need EPIC.

faqs.question: I moved to a new city. How to update my voter ID?
faqs.answer: Apply for 'Shifting' or 'Migration': (1) If moving within the same constituency: Apply Form 6A (within same constituency migration) at voters.eci.gov.in or BLO office. (2) If moving to different constituency: Apply Form 6 (new registration in new constituency) at the new location's voters.eci.gov.in. Old registration is cancelled, new one created at new address. Processing: 15-30 days after BLO verification.

faqs.question: What's the minimum age for voter registration?
faqs.answer: 18 years on the qualifying date. If you turn 18 on or before January 1, April 1, July 1, or October 1, you can register for that date's electoral roll. There are 4 qualifying dates per year now. If you're 17, you can't register yet — wait until you turn 18.

faqs.question: Can I have two voter IDs if I moved states?
faqs.answer: NO. One person can have only ONE EPIC (Voter ID) at a time. If you register in a new state after moving, your old voter ID in the previous state is automatically cancelled. You cannot have dual voter IDs — this is illegal.

faqs.question: My EPIC details are wrong (name/age/address). How to correct?
faqs.answer: Apply for 'Correction of Entry' on voters.eci.gov.in or visit your BLO office with: (1) Application form (available on portal or from BLO), (2) Original document proving correct details (e.g., Aadhaar, passport, 10th marksheet for age), (3) Copy of incorrect EPIC card. Processing: 15-30 days. After correction, download corrected e-EPIC from portal.

faqs.question: I want to register but don't have Aadhaar. Can I still register?
faqs.answer: YES. Aadhaar is NOT mandatory for voter registration. You can use: Birth certificate, 10th marksheet, Passport, Driving License as age proof. For address proof: Utility bill, bank statement, rent agreement, passport — any of these work. Proceed with Form 6 registration using available documents.

disclaimer: Voter registration processes and qualifying dates as per Election Commission of India (ECI) guidelines as of March 2026. Procedures may be updated — always check voters.eci.gov.in for latest information. Electoral roll updates follow ECI's official schedule.
